name: Centro De Apoyo Familiar (Caf) Md
description_short: Non-profit community organization providing free housing counseling, financial literacy, and consumer protection services across Massachusetts, Maryland, Virginia, DC, and Puerto Rico.
description_long:
Centro de Apoyo Familiar (CAF) was founded in 2006 in Lawrence, Massachusetts, initially to provide distressed homeowners with housing counseling services. The organization has expanded significantly, establishing operations in Prince George's County, Maryland (2009), Virginia (2017), Washington DC (2020), and Puerto Rico (2024). CAF operates as a mission-driven non-profit focused on economic, social, and educational empowerment in collaboration with community and faith-based organizations.

CAF offers a comprehensive suite of free and low-cost services organized into four main program areas: Housing and Consumer Programs (foreclosure prevention, homebuyer education, financial literacy, rental counseling, credit counseling, and local consumer protection); Health and Family Services (environmental health, family strengthening, mental health support, and elderly programs); Workforce Development (skill training, digital literacy, CDL training, youth STEM, and small business owner training); and Housing Development with actual property projects. All services are explicitly stated to be accessible to individuals of all backgrounds regardless of race, sex, social or economic status, with compliance to Executive Order 14168.

CAF distinguishes itself through its integrated approach combining housing stability with broader community development and its deep roots in faith-based and community partnerships. The organization operates the CAF Connectors Network specifically for faith-based organizations and demonstrates active community advocacy, as evidenced by leadership participation in public hearings on housing equity. Their programming includes innovative initiatives like community garden projects and hands-on training models.

As a legitimate non-profit, CAF represents genuine free-help resources without predatory lending or debt traps. The main limitation is geographic restriction to five specific regions, and the website provides limited detail on counselor certifications (though housing counseling suggests HUD-approval). Services appear genuinely free-to-low-cost, making this appropriate for low-income families seeking legitimate financial guidance and housing support.
pros: Completely free services with no lending or debt products involved
Multi-state geographic reach across Massachusetts, Maryland, Virginia, DC, and Puerto Rico
Comprehensive program portfolio addressing housing, financial literacy, workforce development, and health
Explicit commitment to serving all populations regardless of race, sex, or economic status
Active community advocacy and partnership with faith-based organizations
Foreclosure prevention and homebuyer education programs with counseling support
Integrated workforce development including CDL training and digital literacy programs
cons: Limited geographic service area—only available in 5 specific regions, excluding most of the country
Website does not clearly specify HUD certification or NFCC accreditation status for counselors
No online application or intake process visible; requires contacting organization directly
Limited information about wait times or capacity constraints for counseling services
Website content appears to have future-dated posts (e.g., events listed for Feb-2026), suggesting outdated maintenance
best_for: Homeowners in Massachusetts, Maryland, Virginia, DC, or Puerto Rico facing foreclosure or seeking loan modification help
First-time homebuyers in served regions seeking education and financial preparation
Low-income families needing free financial literacy education and credit counseling
Individuals seeking workforce development, digital literacy, or small business training in partnered communities
diagnosis: CAF is best for low-to-moderate income families in Massachusetts, Maryland, Virginia, Washington DC, or Puerto Rico seeking completely free, non-predatory housing counseling, financial literacy education, and workforce development support. The primary caveat is strict geographic limitation—this organization cannot help consumers outside its five service regions, and the website should be verified for current program availability and counselor certification status before contacting.
services: Commercial Driver's License (CDL Class B) training
Community garden training and environmental justice programs
Family strengthening and mental health support
typical_results_timeline: Counseling available within 1-2 weeks of contact.
